steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice B
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (-x^9 -13x -6):
Compute the given expression: $$(6x^{3}-6x+4)-(7x^{2}+7x-2) = 6x^{3}-7x^{2}-13x+6$$.
Choice B is $$-x^{9}-13x-6$$; it includes an $$x^{9}$$ term, whereas the simplified expression has highest degree 3, so the two cannot be identical.
Fails.

steps.title: Testing choice C
steps.content:
Testing choice C (-7x^2+6x^3-13x+6):
Distribute the minus sign and combine like terms: $$(6x^{3}-6x+4)-(7x^{2}+7x-2) = 6x^{3}-7x^{2}-13x+6$$.
Reordering terms gives $$-7x^{2}+6x^{3}-13x+6$$, which is exactly choice C.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
